comps.name: Quash — Renais Core
comps.summary: The standard all-content team. Renais is a self-contained counter engine; everyone else just needs to keep enemies attacking her.
comps.whyItWorks: Renais's real engine is [Quash], not [Holy Seal]: every attack and counter stacks [Quash] on the target, and she deals +25% ATK × [Quash] stacks plus +30% Crit Rate against Quash'd enemies. Her passive turns her Basic Attack into Divine Spear Verdict and fires it whenever an ally is hit — so the more the enemy acts, the more she counters and the harder she hits. She sits in the midline for the Star Gate crit-rate buffs. The endgame version surrounds her with 5 Otherworld URs: they keep the full 6× Sanctuary race bonus (Otherworld is a wildcard), sit outside the counter wheel, and bring control so enemies keep feeding her counters. The budget version uses a Sanctuary light team where Michael exploits the [Holy Seal] Renais also applies (Apotheosis → cooldown refresh + True DMG).
comps.mirrorNote: Renais wants counter/crit mirrors — open her hero page for the full ranking.

slots.why: AOE damage reduction + team heal keeps the line alive; as Otherworld she still completes the 6× Sanctuary race bonus.
The engine. Counters every hit on your team, stacking [Quash] for +dmg and +30% crit; midline gives her the Star Gate crit buffs.
AOE execute + [Eternal Curse]; second beneficiary of the midline crit buffs.
Backline AOE execute that finishes targets Renais and Lucifer soften.
AOE [Charm] turns enemy turns into your turns — and charmed enemies still trigger Renais's counters.
Premium control + spell pressure; stacks with Enchantress to lock the enemy ultimate cycle.
Drops enemy ATK and holds the front. She's Arcane Web, costing one race tier — swap to a Sanctuary guard or Jeanne when available.
The engine. Counters every hit, stacking [Quash] for +dmg and +30% crit; midline crit buffs apply.
Exploits [Holy Seal] Renais applies: Apotheosis stacks refresh cooldowns and add True DMG. True damage ignores most defense.
AOE spell + debuffs; also applies [Holy Seal] to feed Michael.
AOE burst caster that cleans up the softened enemy line.
Shields and blessings keep Renais alive so the counter engine never stops.

comps.name: Infect — Ling Core
comps.summary: Spread [Infect] across the whole enemy team to feed Ling free Ultimates. Snowballs harder the wider the infection.
comps.whyItWorks: Ying Gou is the applier — her skill scatters 4–6 stacks of [Infect] across mid/backline enemies. Ling is the exploiter: her Ultimate adds True DMG (120% ATK) to infected targets, and she gains [Battle Will] for every infected enemy (max 6/turn), triggering a free Ultimate at 8 stacks and again at 14. So the plan is wide, fast infection → Ling chain-ults. Orpheus adds %Max-HP damage to infected targets as a third exploiter. The budget version is mono Arcane Web (full 6× race bonus); the endgame version keeps the Ling + Ying Gou applier pair and fills the rest with Otherworld URs for the wildcard race bonus and control.
comps.mirrorNote: Ling and Ying Gou want [Infect]-gated mirrors — see their hero pages.

slots.why: Survivable Otherworld front that completes the 6× Arcane Web race bonus.
The engine. Converts team-wide [Infect] into free Ultimates ([Battle Will] at 8/14 stacks).
AOE execute that capitalizes on enemies softened by Infect True DMG; takes the midline crit buffs.
The fuel. Scatters 4–6 [Infect] stacks per cast across mid/back — keeps Ling's [Battle Will] topped up.
Adds %Max-HP damage to [Infect] targets; a second source of infection payoff.
AOE [Charm] keeps the enemy alive-but-helpless so the infection ticks and Ling keeps ulting.
Arcane Web taunt + damage-share guard; keeps the whole team mono-faction for the full 6× bonus.
The engine. Free Ultimates from team-wide [Infect]; midline crit buffs apply.
Counter-burst that adds %Max-HP damage to infected enemies.
The fuel — 4–6 [Infect] stacks per cast across the enemy back line.
Lilith adds more [Infect] plus kill-detonation, widening the infection and cleaning low-HP targets.
Arcane Web healer that keeps the squishy casters alive without breaking the race bonus.

comps.name: Bleed — Alucard Core
comps.summary: Spread [Bleed] across the enemy team, then let Alucard rake every bleeding target each turn.
comps.whyItWorks: Sherry is the AOE applier (70% [Bleed] to all enemies); Alucard is the engine — he applies [Bleed] on the lowest-DEF target (100%), gains [Bloodlust] per application, and re-hits ALL bleeding enemies with [Vacuum Blade] every active turn. The more enemies bleeding, the more his Vacuum Blade hits. The budget version is mono Kindred (full 6× race bonus); the endgame version keeps the Alucard + Sherry pair and fills with Otherworld URs.
comps.mirrorNote: Alucard scales with crit and execute mirrors — see his hero page.

slots.why: Otherworld front that completes the 6× Kindred race bonus.
The engine. Re-hits every bleeding enemy with [Vacuum Blade] each turn; midline crit buffs apply.
AOE execute that finishes bleeding, softened targets.
Mass [Bleed] applier (70% to all) and team healer — fuels Alucard's whole-team rake.
AOE execute + curse; layers more pressure on the bleeding line.
AOE [Charm] keeps enemies in place to keep bleeding.
Kindred counter-guard; keeps the team mono-faction for the full 6× bonus.
The engine. [Vacuum Blade] rakes every bleeding enemy each turn.
AOE burst + shield breaker to crack tanky enemy fronts.
Mass [Bleed] + AOE heal — the fuel and the sustain in one slot.
AOE control keeps enemies locked while they bleed out.
Low-HP execute that closes out targets the bleed has chipped.

comps.name: Sigil — Demonhunter Core
comps.summary: Adele and Rose blanket the enemy in [Sigil]; the Demonhunter board converts it into burst.
comps.whyItWorks: Adele and Rose are the [Sigil] appliers (50–80% chance across their multi-hits), and Rose's passive triggers extra ally Basic Attacks on kills — so a Demonhunter board keeps acting and re-applying. Exploiters like Uncle Ying gain [Amulet] off [Sigil] application, and the Sigil payoff scales through the rest of the Demonhunter roster. The budget version is mono Demonhunter (full 6× race bonus); the endgame version keeps Adele + Rose and fills with Otherworld URs.
comps.mirrorNote: Adele and Rose want [Sigil]/crit mirrors — see their hero pages.

slots.why: Otherworld front that completes the 6× Demonhunter race bonus.
Core DPS + [Sigil] applier; the midline crit buffs power her multi-hit bursts.
Wu Kong's clone assault adds a second crit-scaling hitter in the crit lane.
Rose blankets [Sigil] and triggers extra ally Basic Attacks on kills — the action-economy fuel.
AOE execute that converts the softened, Sigil'd enemy line into kills.
AOE [Charm] buys the Demonhunter board the turns it needs to stack [Sigil].
Garvin's AOE shield + shield curse holds the front and keeps the team mono-Demonhunter for the 6× bonus.
Core DPS + [Sigil] applier; midline crit buffs power her bursts.
Van Helsing first-strikes and single-kills priority targets in the crit lane.
Rose blankets [Sigil] and fuels extra ally Basic Attacks on kills.
Uncle Ying gains [Amulet] off [Sigil] application and adds AOE magic burst.
Theresa's AOE heal + dispel keeps the board alive and clean.

comps.name: Otherworld — Pure UR Core
comps.summary: Six Otherworld URs: the dedicated 6× Otherworld bonus (with Final DMG) and zero faction-counter risk. The whale endgame default.
comps.whyItWorks: Fielding 6 Otherworld heroes triggers the dedicated race tier — ATK +20% / DEF +15% / HP +30% AND Final DMG +15% — and because every hero is Otherworld, the team sits entirely outside the faction-counter wheel (it can never be Strong-Struck). With no SSR+ carry to protect, every slot is a maxed UR, so this is pure stat-and-kit power: a tank, two crit DPS in the mid lane, and a control+execute back line. It needs deep UR investment, which is why it's the endgame/whale default rather than a budget option.
comps.mirrorNote: Each UR has its own mirror ranking — open their hero pages.

slots.why: Gaia's lethal-immunity + Spirit Armor make her the sturdiest Otherworld front.
Nyx's AOE execute scales with the midline crit buffs.
Lucifer's AOE execute + curse shares the crit lane and the Final DMG bonus.
Enchantress's AOE [Charm] is the premier lockdown — supreme control.
Venus stacks more control + spell pressure to freeze the enemy ult cycle.
Mars's multi-hit + debuff control adds sustained backline damage.
